code: @Manual{,   title = {jlview: Zero-Copy Julia to R Array Bridge via ALTREP},   author = {Aviezer Lifshitz},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/tanaylab/jlview}, }

what: Zero-Copy Julia to R Array Bridge via ALTREP
title: Zero-Copy Julia to R Array Bridge via ALTREP
text: jlview provides zero-copy R views Julia-owned arrays using R’s ALTREP (Alternative Representations) framework. Instead copying data Julia R, jlview returns lightweight R vectors point directly Julia’s memory. Benchmark: 10K × 1K named Float64 matrix

title: Zero-Copy Julia to R Array Bridge via ALTREP
Zero-Copy Julia to R Array Bridge via ALTREP
text: R ≥ 4.0 Julia ≥ 1.6 JuliaCall R package

title: Zero-Copy Julia to R Array Bridge via ALTREP
code: library(jlview) JuliaCall::julia_setup()  # Create a Julia array JuliaCall::julia_command("x = randn(10000, 1000)")  # Zero-copy view — R sees Julia's memory directly m <- jlview(JuliaCall::julia_eval("x")) dim(m)    # [1] 10000  1000 sum(m)    # works natively, no data copied  # Named arrays are supported JuliaCall::julia_command("using NamedArrays") JuliaCall::julia_command("named = NamedArray(randn(3), [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"])") v <- jlview_named_vector(JuliaCall::julia_eval("named")) v["a"]    # access by name, still zero-copy  # Sparse matrices (zero-copy values, shifted indices) JuliaCall::julia_command("using SparseArrays") JuliaCall::julia_command("sp = sprand(1000, 500, 0.01)") s <- jlview_sparse(JuliaCall::julia_eval("sp")) class(s)  # "dgCMatrix"  # Explicit release to free Julia memory early jlview_release(m)

title: Zero-Copy Julia to R Array Bridge via ALTREP
text: Zero-copy dense arrays — Float64, Int32 map directly R’s REALSXP, INTSXP Type conversion — Float32, Int64, Int16 converted Julia, zero-copy R Named arrays — NamedArray row/column names attached atomically without triggering copy Sparse matrices — dgCMatrix zero-copy values (nzval) shifted indices Copy--write — R’s standard COW semantics: reads zero-copy, writes trigger materialization GC safety — Julia arrays pinned R holds references; three-layer defense (pinning dict, memory pressure tracking, explicit release) Fork safety — Safe parallel::mclapply() (PID-guarded finalizers) Serialization — saveRDS()/readRDS() work correctly (materializes save)

title: Zero-Copy Julia to R Array Bridge via ALTREP
text: jlview uses R’s ALTREP framework create R vectors backed Julia memory: Pin — Julia array stored global dictionary, preventing Julia’s GC collecting Wrap — ALTREP R vector created whose Dataptr returns Julia’s raw data pointer Use — R operations (sum, subsetting, etc.) read directly Julia’s memory Release — R garbage-collects ALTREP object, C finalizer calls Julia unpin array entire pin→ALTREP→finalizer path implemented C using Julia’s C API (jl_call1), avoiding JuliaCall overhead ensuring safety R’s garbage collection.

what: Check if an object is a jlview ALTREP vector — is_jlview
title: Check if an object is a jlview ALTREP vector — is_jlview
text: Check object jlview ALTREP vector

title: Check if an object is a jlview ALTREP vector — is_jlview
Check if an object is a jlview ALTREP vector — is_jlview
text: x R object

title: Check if an object is a jlview ALTREP vector — is_jlview
text: TRUE x jlview ALTREP vector, FALSE otherwise

what: Create a zero-copy R view of a Julia array — jlview
title: Create a zero-copy R view of a Julia array — jlview
text: Creates ALTREP vector (matrix, 2D+) backed Julia memory. resulting R object shares memory Julia array, avoiding data copying. Modifications R object trigger copy--write (unless writeable = TRUE).

title: Create a zero-copy R view of a Julia array — jlview
code: jlview(julia_array, writeable = FALSE, names = NULL, dimnames = NULL)

title: Create a zero-copy R view of a Julia array — jlview
text: julia_array JuliaObject referencing Julia array writeable TRUE, allow R write directly Julia's memory. Use caution — enables shared mutation. Default FALSE. names Optional character vector names attach result. Attached atomically construction avoid ALTREP materialization. dimnames Optional list dimnames attach result. Attached atomically construction avoid ALTREP materialization.

title: Create a zero-copy R view of a Julia array — jlview
text: ALTREP vector backed Julia memory, standard R vector Julia type supported zero-copy.

title: Create a zero-copy R view of a Julia array — jlview
code: if (interactive()) {     JuliaCall::julia_setup()     # Create a Julia array and view it in R without copying     JuliaCall::julia_command("x = randn(1000)")     x <- jlview(JuliaCall::julia_eval("x"))     sum(x) # operates directly on Julia memory }

title: Get current GC pressure information — jlview_gc_pressure
text: Returns current amount Julia memory pinned jlview objects threshold forced GC triggered.

title: Get current GC pressure information — jlview_gc_pressure
Get current GC pressure information — jlview_gc_pressure
text: list pinned_bytes threshold

what: Get information about a jlview object — jlview_info
title: Get information about a jlview object — jlview_info
text: Returns metadata jlview ALTREP vector including Julia element type, length, writeability, release status.

title: Get information about a jlview object — jlview_info
Get information about a jlview object — jlview_info
text: x jlview ALTREP vector

title: Get information about a jlview object — jlview_info
text: named list components: type Julia element type (e.g., "Float64") length Number elements writeable Whether view allows direct writes released Whether view released materialized Whether COW materialization occurred

what: Create a zero-copy R view of a named Julia matrix — jlview_named_matrix
title: Create a zero-copy R view of a named Julia matrix — jlview_named_matrix
text: Creates zero-copy ALTREP view Julia NamedArray matrix, preserving row column names dimnames.

title: Create a zero-copy R view of a named Julia matrix — jlview_named_matrix
Create a zero-copy R view of a named Julia matrix — jlview_named_matrix
text: julia_named_matrix JuliaObject referencing Julia NamedArray matrix

title: Create a zero-copy R view of a named Julia matrix — jlview_named_matrix
text: ALTREP matrix dimnames set Julia NamedArray

title: Create a zero-copy R view of a named Julia matrix — jlview_named_matrix
code: if (interactive()) {     JuliaCall::julia_setup()     JuliaCall::julia_command("using NamedArrays")     m <- JuliaCall::julia_eval('NamedArray(randn(3,2), (["a","b","c"], ["x","y"]))')     x <- jlview_named_matrix(m)     rownames(x) # returns c("a", "b", "c")     colnames(x) # returns c("x", "y") }

what: Create a zero-copy R view of a named Julia vector — jlview_named_vector
title: Create a zero-copy R view of a named Julia vector — jlview_named_vector
text: Creates zero-copy ALTREP view Julia NamedArray vector, preserving axis names.

title: Create a zero-copy R view of a named Julia vector — jlview_named_vector
Create a zero-copy R view of a named Julia vector — jlview_named_vector
text: julia_named_array JuliaObject referencing Julia NamedArray vector

title: Create a zero-copy R view of a named Julia vector — jlview_named_vector
text: ALTREP vector names set Julia NamedArray

title: Create a zero-copy R view of a named Julia vector — jlview_named_vector
code: if (interactive()) {     JuliaCall::julia_setup()     JuliaCall::julia_command("using NamedArrays")     v <- JuliaCall::julia_eval('NamedArray([1.0, 2.0, 3.0], (["a", "b", "c"],))')     x <- jlview_named_vector(v)     names(x) # returns c("a", "b", "c") }

what: Explicitly release a jlview object — jlview_release
title: Explicitly release a jlview object — jlview_release
text: Unpins Julia array immediately, freeing memory without waiting R's garbage collector. release, accessing data error.

title: Explicitly release a jlview object — jlview_release
Explicitly release a jlview object — jlview_release
text: x jlview ALTREP vector

title: Explicitly release a jlview object — jlview_release
text: Invisible NULL

what: Set the GC pressure threshold — jlview_set_gc_threshold
title: Set the GC pressure threshold — jlview_set_gc_threshold
text: total pinned bytes exceeds threshold, jlview forces R garbage collection reclaim stale ALTREP objects. Default 2GB.

title: Set the GC pressure threshold — jlview_set_gc_threshold
Set the GC pressure threshold — jlview_set_gc_threshold
text: bytes Threshold bytes (numeric)

title: Set the GC pressure threshold — jlview_set_gc_threshold
text: Invisible NULL

what: Create a zero-copy R sparse matrix view of a Julia SparseMatrixCSC — jlview_sparse
title: Create a zero-copy R sparse matrix view of a Julia SparseMatrixCSC — jlview_sparse
text: Creates dgCMatrix-class backed zero-copy ALTREP vectors nonzero values (x slot) ALTREP index vectors row indices (slot) column pointers (p slot). Julia--R index shift (1-based 0-based) handled lazily ALTREP index class.

title: Create a zero-copy R sparse matrix view of a Julia SparseMatrixCSC — jlview_sparse
Create a zero-copy R sparse matrix view of a Julia SparseMatrixCSC — jlview_sparse
text: julia_sparse_matrix JuliaObject referencing Julia SparseMatrixCSC. value type must supported zero-copy (Float64, Float32, Int32, Int64, Int16, UInt8). lazy_indices FALSE (default), index vectors (p) eagerly materialized standard R integer vectors construction. avoids repeated lazy -1 shifts every element access recommended matrices accessed many times. TRUE, indices remain lazy ALTREP views compute shift --fly.

title: Create a zero-copy R sparse matrix view of a Julia SparseMatrixCSC — jlview_sparse
text: dgCMatrix-class sparse matrix.

title: Create a zero-copy R sparse matrix view of a Julia SparseMatrixCSC — jlview_sparse
code: if (interactive()) {     JuliaCall::julia_setup()     JuliaCall::julia_command("using SparseArrays")     m <- JuliaCall::julia_eval("sprand(Float64, 100, 50, 0.1)")     s <- jlview_sparse(m)     class(s) # "dgCMatrix" }

what: Use a jlview object within a scope, releasing it on exit — with_jlview
title: Use a jlview object within a scope, releasing it on exit — with_jlview
text: Creates jlview object ensures released scope exits, even error occurs. prevents memory leaks forgotten releases.

title: Use a jlview object within a scope, releasing it on exit — with_jlview
code: with_jlview(   julia_array,   expr,   writeable = FALSE,   names = NULL,   dimnames = NULL )

title: Use a jlview object within a scope, releasing it on exit — with_jlview
text: julia_array JuliaObject referencing Julia array expr expression evaluate jlview object bound .x writeable Passed jlview names Passed jlview dimnames Passed jlview

title: Use a jlview object within a scope, releasing it on exit — with_jlview
text: result evaluating expr

title: Use a jlview object within a scope, releasing it on exit — with_jlview
code: if (interactive()) {     JuliaCall::julia_setup()     JuliaCall::julia_command("big = randn(100000)")     result <- with_jlview(JuliaCall::julia_eval("big"), {         c(mean(.x), sd(.x))     })     # .x is automatically released here }

title: jlview 0.1.0
text: Initial release.

title: jlview 0.1.0
text: Zero-copy R views Julia Float64 Int32 arrays via ALTREP Automatic type conversion Float32, Int64, Int16, UInt8 arrays Sparse matrix support (dgCMatrix) zero-copy nonzero values Named vector matrix support atomic dimnames (COW) Scope-based release with_jlview() ALTREP Sum/Min/Max methods calling Julia directly performance GC pressure tracking configurable threshold Safe cross-runtime garbage collection via C-level finalizers Fork safety (mclapply), Julia shutdown safety, double-release protection Full serialization support (saveRDS/readRDS)
